The PR updates the vendored `npm-shrinkwrap.json` entry for `node_modules/hono` from `4.12.18` to `4.12.23`.

PR surface: Other 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and `v2026.6.1` pin `hono@4.12.18`, and GitHub Advisory API marks Hono versions below `4.12.21` as vulnerable; I did not run the downstream `npm audit` install path in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(npm-shrinkwrap): update hono from 4.12.18 to 4.12.23 to fix security vulnerabilities (#91301)
target.body:
## Summary

Updates `npm-shrinkwrap.json` to resolve `hono@4.12.18` to `hono@4.12.23`, addressing 4 moderate security vulnerabilities reported in issue #91301.

## Changes

- Updated `node_modules/hono` from `4.12.18` to `4.12.23` in `npm-shrinkwrap.json`

## Security advisories fixed

- GHSA-xrhx-7g5j-rcj5
- GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x
- GHSA-f577-qrjj-4474
- GHSA-2gCR-mfcq-wcc3

All advisories list first patched version as `4.12.21`; this PR updates to `4.12.23` (latest available).

## Verification

- `npm-shrinkwrap.json` updated with correct integrity hash for `hono@4.12.23`
- Dependency range `^4.11.4` from `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0` satisfied by `4.12.23`

## Real behavior proof

N/A - This is a dependency version update that will be verified by downstream `npm audit` runs. The fix is straightforward:
- Before: `hono@4.12.18` (vulnerable)
- After: `hono@4.12.23` (patched)

Downstream users can verify by running `npm audit` after installing this version of OpenClaw.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm-shrinkwrap.json` updated with correct version and integrity hash
- [ ] Downstream `npm audit` passes (requires publish/install)
